hard.next_step: 6/26mc- Oscar did not make it happen to our meeting yesterday. Reeschedule the call for this upcoming thursday. SC assigned into the account. Sending Oscar some of the new translated spanish material to keep the momentum going.
hard.ais_why:
• SuKarne has the full agentic suite scoped (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) across an eProc + Spend + Intake motion.
• No discovery description, pain points, or agentic-platform discussion are captured, so buyer sentiment toward AI is unproven.
• Product breadth suggests AI is in scope, but the absence of conversation evidence keeps appetite in the curious range with low confidence.
hard.primary_competitor: Do Nothing / SAP Ariba status quo

north_star_verdict.headline: Genuinely interested procurement head Oscar owns a purchase-automation mandate and pulled the next meeting himself, but he no-showed the 23 Jun follow-up and the rep is now chasing; the deal is single-threaded with the real budget owner (the family owner above Oscar) never contacted.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 10 Jun 2026, Qualified since 13 Jun, ~26 days old. Close date 30 Jun 2027 is ~359 days out. No Qualified/Formal_Eval/Shortlisted submission dates on record, so stage velocity cannot be computed from the submission series. Only one buyer touch is a genuine two-way exchange (intro call 10 Jun); the 23 Jun follow-up was a buyer no-show, rescheduled to ~2 Jul per Next_Step 26 Jun, with no recorded outcome as of 6 Jul. A 12-month LATAM enterprise cycle is credible ONLY if discovery, multi-threading to the budget owner, a scoped evaluation, commercials, and contracting all execute without delay; current pace (one call, one no-show, rep sending translated materials to keep momentum) does not yet support that. Forecast Pipeline is correct.
evidence: Intro call 10 Jun 2026: Oscar articulated a clear transformation mandate, from transactional (invoice-to-pay, PO-to-pay) to a procurement center of excellence focused on category management, spend management and strategic sourcing (source: Avoma 10 Jun, Oscar Felix Chaidez).

Oscar himself requested the next meeting and committed to present his 'automation of purchases' project: 'que te parece si hacemos otra llamada en la siguiente semana, donde yo te exponga todo lo que quiero hacer de automatizacion de compras' (source: Avoma 10 Jun, Oscar).

The 23 Jun follow-up was a buyer no-show; the rep rescheduled to the following Thursday and is sending translated Spanish materials 'to keep the momentum going' (source: SF Next_Step 26 Jun, LastActivityDate 23 Jun) - a rep-side chase awaiting a buyer reply, not buyer engagement.

Single-threaded on Oscar; the true budget authority is the second-generation family owner he answers to ('cuando el jefe llama, tengo que contestar'), who has never been contacted (source: Avoma 10 Jun, Oscar).

Incumbent SAP Ariba is installed (contracts, guided sourcing) but admittedly underused for lack of training and focus; Oscar's original plan was to hand the automation project to an SAP partner (source: Avoma 10 Jun, Oscar).
No economic buyer engaged, no commercial/pricing/security/legal discussion, no decision process or approval chain mapped, no quantified value case (gaps per MEDDPICC synthesis).

metrics.narrative: The business problem is concrete: SuKarne's procurement is stuck in transactional invoice-to-pay and PO-to-pay with no category management, spend management, strategic sourcing or demand planning, which Oscar wants to move to a center of excellence (Avoma 10 Jun, Oscar: 'quizas hoy somos... transaccionales... no tenemos sourcing'). Oscar confirmed the highest-volume pain is repetitive status queries when the rep cited 40%+ of team time lost to non-value follow-ups ('No, mucho, si... atender preguntas con estatus, si, mucho'). No dollar, headcount, cycle-time or savings figure was ever quantified, so the value case is qualitative only. Quantifying the cost of inaction (status-query hours, spend leakage from no category management) is the next move.
sources: Avoma discovery call 10 Jun 2026 (Oscar Felix Chaidez)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped in Salesforce (no OpportunityContactRole; Oscar is only a task/activity contact). Oscar is the senior procurement leader who owns the 'automation of purchases' project and is the closest thing to a budget influencer, but he explicitly answers to a boss: he interrupted the call for the owner ('cuando el jefe llama, tengo que contestar') and described SuKarne as an owner-run second-generation family business. For a $150k+ enterprise commitment the true budget authority is that family owner (or finance) above Oscar, and that person has never been contacted. Identifying and reaching the budget owner through Oscar is a ranked early-stage move.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are inferred from the discovery call, not yet formally documented. Oscar values: fit with SuKarne's existing Google/Gemini environment ('veo mucho valor en utilizar Google, si esta conectado con Gemini'), ease of use and low training overhead (he blamed Ariba's underuse on lack of training), native integration with their SAP S/4HANA + Ariba stack, and coverage of the maturity gaps he named (category management, spend management, strategic sourcing, procurement automation). No formal RFI/RFP, business-requirement document, or weighted criteria set has been shared. Confirming written evaluation criteria is a discovery-depth move for the next call.

decision_process.narrative: No formal evaluation or approval process has been established. Oscar frames this as his internal 'automation of purchases' project, which he originally intended to route through an SAP partner ('yo habia pensado que ibamos a ir a buscar un partner de SAP... para que ese partner haga todo'). He also flagged a self-imposed prerequisite: he must first fix internal category-management data before adopting new tooling ('necesito hacer un trabajo previo'). No buying committee, timeline, or approval chain is mapped. Reading the call sequence, only Oscar has appeared; mapping who else decides (IT/SAP owner, finance, the owner) is the process gap to close.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced - this is a 26-day-old net-new deal still in early discovery. As a new-business full-suite deal it would eventually require an MSA, Order Form and a separately-signed SOW, plus InfoSec (SOC/DPA) given the SAP/Google data environment, but none of that has been discussed. Current_Contract_Expiration is not on record, so there is no contract-driven forcing function yet. Visibility here would come only once an evaluation is scoped and commercials open; it is not a live gate at this stage.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated and owned by the relevant stakeholder described procurement as immature and 'atrapados en el ' (trapped in transactional work), with no category management, no strategic sourcing and no demand planning, coming from a more mature Cinepolis environment. He confirmed heavy repetitive status-query load on the team and admitted Ariba's contracts/guided-sourcing/supplier modules are enabled but underused for lack of training and focus ('tenemos Ariba... pero no sabemos usarlo, es falta de entrenamiento'). The pain is tied to a strategic mandate to modernize, which makes it real rather than idle. The pain owner is Oscar; the risk is that fixing it competes with his own prerequisite internal cleanup and other priorities.

champion.narrative: Oscar Felix Chaidez is a developing but fragile champion. What has developed: he took the intro call, disclosed real pain and strategy, pulled the next meeting himself, and committed to present his automation project - genuine buyer-initiated interest. What is missing: he no-showed the 23 Jun follow-up (rescheduled to ~2 Jul, no recorded outcome), he is the only contact (single-thread), and his access to the budget owner above him is unproven. He is engaged but not yet tested as an internal seller. The next call is the gate: a clear scoped project plus agreement to a demo/pilot upgrades him to a real champion; another slip degrades him to weak.

competition.narrative: The strongest current threat is do-nothing inertia: Oscar admits procurement is immature and that he must first fix internal category-management data before advancing any tool, so status-quo delay is the real risk, not a named rival. The incumbent is SAP Ariba (contracts, guided sourcing, supplier database on S/4HANA), installed but underused - a medium threat because it 'keeps the department standing' yet frustrates Oscar. Oscar's original path was to engage an SAP partner to custom-build the automation on SAP + Google, a live services-led alternative. SAP Jules (SAP's procurement AI agent) is dormant: Oscar declined early-adopter status during the S/4HANA migration but asked to be re-invited in H2 2026. The wedge is Zycus's Google Chat / Merlin agentic layer that sits over SAP, aligns with SuKarne's Gemini rollout, and solves the intake/status-query pain Ariba does not - proven live before Jules re-engages in H2 2026.

deal_movement.summary: Net-new deal created 10 Jun 2026 and qualified 13 Jun. Amount was sized 200k -> 210k -> 150k on 13 Jun (initial deal-shaping, not a buyer-driven cut). Ownership moved from Mario Castro to Karson Keogh on 26 Jun; division moved to New Markets 24 Jun. Buyer touch: rich intro call 10 Jun, then a 23 Jun no-show rescheduled to ~2 Jul. The genuine last two-way exchange with the buyer is the 10 Jun call; everything since is rep-side chasing awaiting Oscar's reply.

items.change: Intro discovery call held with Oscar; pain and automation mandate captured
Buyer no-showed the follow-up meeting; rep rescheduled to ~2 Jul
Ownership reassigned Mario Castro -> Karson Keogh

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, all evidence from the 10 Jun 2026 intro call. The single strongest CURRENT threat is DO-NOTHING inertia: Oscar admits procurement is immature and that he must complete internal category-management cleanup before adopting any tool, so status-quo delay is the live risk. SAP Ariba is the installed incumbent (contracts, guided sourcing, supplier database) but underused for lack of training - a medium threat that props up the status quo rather than actively competing. An SAP-partner services-led build was Oscar's original plan (medium). SAP Jules is dormant, declined during the migration with a requested H2-2026 revisit (low). No independent best-of-breed rival (Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, Zip) has been named.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / status quo
competitors.quote: Oscar: procurement is immature and 'atrapados en el ', and 'necesito hacer un trabajo previo' before advancing - a self-imposed internal prerequisite that can stall any purchase.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of inaction (status-query hours, spend leakage from no category management) and tie the automation project to a near-term quick win so modernizing beats waiting; make the mandate undeniable rather than optional.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent)
competitors.quote: Oscar: 'tenemos Ariba... pero no sabemos usarlo, es falta de entrenamiento' and 'esta bien, es que no estamos enfocados, estamos atrapados' - contracts and guided sourcing enabled but underused.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the automation and intake layer over SAP, not a rip-and-replace: native SAP integration plus a Google Chat / Merlin agent that solves the status-query problem Ariba never addressed, unlocking adoption via a familiar training-free interface.

competitors.name: SAP partner (services-led build)
competitors.quote: Oscar: 'yo habia pensado que ibamos a ir a buscar un partner de SAP... para que ese partner haga todo, tanto SAP como automatizaciones en Google.'
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast a proven enterprise platform with Merlin agentic AI against a custom services build that carries longer timelines, higher implementation risk and ongoing services spend; offer a pilot to prove faster time-to-value than a partner project.

competitors.name: SAP Jules
competitors.quote: Oscar: 'Nos invitaron a ser early adopters de Jules... les dije que no, no estamos listos, pero les pedi que nos invitaran el segundo semestre 2026.'
competitors.how_we_win: Land and prove Merlin value before SAP re-engages with Jules in H2 2026; position Merlin as procurement-purpose-built and aligned with SuKarne's Gemini/Google environment, whereas Jules deepens SAP-only lock-in.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit signals are early but favourable on the differentiators Oscar cares about. He explicitly values alignment with SuKarne's Google/Gemini environment and low training overhead, both Zycus strengths via the Google Chat / Merlin agent, but native SAP integration and full source-to-pay maturity still need to be proven against his immature data.

Only Oscar engaged; no IT/SAP owner, finance, or owner contacted

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for this early, single-threaded LATAM deal, most important first.
flags: Multi-thread beyond Oscar to the family owner/finance budget authority and the SAP/IT owner before the automation project firms up - the deal has no path to a $150k+ sign-off today.
Land the live Merlin Google Chat agent against the status-query pain to differentiate from underused Ariba and pre-empt SAP Jules (H2 2026) and the SAP-partner services path.
Convert do-nothing inertia by quantifying the cost of inaction and tying it to a near-term category-management quick win, since Oscar's own prerequisite cleanup is the real stall risk.

items.name: Oscar Miguel Felix Chaidez
items.title: Head of Procurement (rep addressed him as CPO on 10 Jun; no Salesforce title on record)
items.risk: Single point of contact; no-showed the 23 Jun follow-up; access to the budget owner above him unproven

champion_strength.summary: Oscar Miguel Felix Chaidez is a developing but fragile champion. He disclosed real pain and a modernization mandate on 10 Jun, pulled the next meeting himself, and committed to present his automation project - genuine buyer-initiated interest. But he no-showed the 23 Jun follow-up (rescheduled to ~2 Jul, no recorded outcome), he is the sole contact, and his access to the budget owner above him is unproven. The next call decides whether he strengthens to a real internal seller or degrades to weak.
champion_strength.source: Avoma 10 Jun 2026; SF Next_Step 26 Jun (no-show 23 Jun)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic story landed reasonably on the intro call: Mario positioned Merlin as a Google Chat control-tower agent, and Oscar responded that he saw genuine value given SuKarne's Gemini/Google adoption. It is a single overview, not a deep AI evaluation, so positioning is present but not yet proven with a live demo.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 5.0 / Moderate on scoped agentic breadth (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) with no conversation evidence in fields. The 10 Jun call actually shows more appetite than the field implies: SuKarne is mid-rollout of Gemini/Google Workspace and Oscar explicitly saw value in an agentic Google Chat interface, so AI curiosity is real but unqualified. Slightly under-rated versus the calls given the Gemini alignment.

items.detail: Single-threaded on Oscar; the true budget owner (the second-generation family owner / finance) is unmapped and never contacted
Procurement is immature; Oscar says internal category-management cleanup must happen first, so do-nothing/inertia can stall the deal
Oscar's original plan was to route the automation build through an SAP partner (services-led alternative to a platform)
Deep SAP S/4HANA + Ariba environment; Zycus must prove native SAP integration and clean data normalization on immature data

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling on the buyer side: the last genuine two-way exchange was the 10 Jun intro call, Oscar no-showed the 23 Jun follow-up, and the rep is chasing with translated materials without a confirmed reply. One good early call, but single-threaded and unconverted.

Only one buyer contact (Oscar) and one buyer call on record

scope_change.to: eProc + Spend + Intake full agentic suite, ~$150k
scope_change.detail: Amount moved 200k -> 210k -> 150k on 13 Jun during initial deal-shaping at qualification, not a buyer-driven defensive narrowing; product scope remains the full suite.

customer_preference.evidence: Oscar saw genuine value in Zycus aligning with SuKarne's Google/Gemini environment ('veo mucho valor en utilizar Google, si esta conectado con Gemini') but did not express a decisive lean and is still in learning mode ('no estoy seguro como Zycus me puede ayudar').

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only - procurement immaturity, heavy repetitive status queries, lack of visibility - with no ROI, savings, or cost figure quantified.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Net-new Qualified deal with a genuinely interested procurement head (Oscar) who owns a purchase-automation mandate and pulled the next meeting himself, but it is a 26-day-old single-threaded intro with the real budget owner (the family owner above him) unmapped, and he just no-showed the follow-up the rep is now chasing.

win_position.text: Oscar, SuKarne's procurement head, has a real modernization mandate and saw genuine fit in Zycus's Google Chat / Gemini-aligned Merlin layer, but this is a single-threaded intro with no economic buyer and no commercial discussion (Avoma, 10 Jun).
Oscar no-showed the 23 Jun follow-up and the rep is chasing with translated materials, so buyer commitment is still unproven (SF Next_Step, 26 Jun).
The true budget owner is the second-generation family owner Oscar answers to ('cuando el jefe llama') and that person has never been contacted (Avoma, 10 Jun).

deal_momentum.text: Oscar himself requested the next meeting and committed to present his 'automation of purchases' project (Avoma, 10 Jun).
Momentum stalled on his 23 Jun no-show, with no confirmed outcome from the ~2 Jul reschedule as of early July (SF activities, 23 Jun).
Oscar said he must first fix internal category-management data before advancing any tool, a self-imposed delay (Avoma, 10 Jun).

customer_commitment.text: Oscar sees strategic value in Zycus aligning with SuKarne's existing Google/Gemini environment ('veo mucho valor en utilizar Google si esta conectado con Gemini', Avoma 10 Jun).
No pricing, security, legal, or timeline has been discussed; engagement is still a single generic overview (Avoma, 10 Jun).
He originally intended to hand the automation project to an SAP partner rather than a platform vendor (Avoma, 10 Jun).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded on Oscar with the economic buyer (the family owner / finance) never engaged (Avoma, 10 Jun).
Incumbent SAP Ariba is installed though underused, and SAP Jules is a deferred H2-2026 alternative Oscar asked to revisit (Avoma, 10 Jun).
Do-nothing inertia is the biggest threat: Oscar admits procurement is immature and prerequisite internal work is needed before any tool advances (Avoma, 10 Jun).

deal_momentum.evidence: Buyer requested the next meeting then no-showed 23 Jun; rep chasing with no confirmed reply.

customer_commitment.evidence: Genuine interest but no commercial/security/timeline discussion; single generic overview.

deal_risk.evidence: Single-thread on Oscar, EB (family owner) unmapped, incumbent SAP Ariba plus do-nothing inertia.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).

commentary.win_position: Win 11 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 49 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (11), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 49, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
